title: Plump Indian Samphire Naan
description: A rich and chewy Maharashtrian dish featuring samphire, perfect for a filling lunch or dinner, this naan is ayurvedic tamasic friendly and made with fry grind rice khao khuah.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine rice flour, fry grind rice khao khuah, and salt.
Add ghee, yogurt, cumin seeds, and coriander powder to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 6 equal portions and roll each portion into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 6 inches in diameter.
Place a few pieces of samphire in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the samphire to form a naan and press the edges together to seal.
Place the naans on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15 minutes.
Brush the naans with ghee and serve hot.
